channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /smg/ - stock market GUNTS — 290 replies — 1160.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/smg/ - stock market GUNTS

Open thread • Replies: 290 • Updated: 2026-03-10 02:39 UTC

OP

Laughing at the market crash from Merida BISH

>Please leave your political posts at the door unless they involve Business or Finance.

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com
https://www.khanacademy.org/economics-finance-domain

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

Previous:
>>61960612

Early replies (first 3)

61961194
>MemoryGODS

61961195
Dip buyers are going to be torn a new asshole soon.

61961196
>b-buy the oil dip guys dont listen to those heckin shills!
>please pump my bags i promise im not a panican!!
Ohh baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61961961
>>61961946
>The strait is totally open guys, feel free to sail through the warzone lol
Adults are posting, young man.

61961967
>>61961948
>to be fair things don't move ina straight line & that was some ridiculous backwardation. the "very complete, pretty much" was a good excuse. imo we'd need to establish beachheads to secure the strait so i'm watchin the dec '26 contract

they would have to control every part of coast within drone distance. Either total war or trump makes a deal that put iranians in much more advantageous position.

>>61961956
these are billion dollar hedge funds, firms and quants who have liquidity to make oil jump so someone is purposely suppressing oil prices under 90 for some reason. >>61961960

61961968
>>61961961
A ship hasn't been droned for 5 days, its hardly a warzone

61961969
>>61961960
>another chuuni larp hesgeth power hour
Oh boy.

61961971
I'm very excited to wake up in the morning and see how much money I've made

61961972
>>61961947
I am not Christian or even Abrahamic, but I definitely think something is up. Though, maybe that is just what it feels like to be outside of a personality cult looking inward.

61961973
>>61961960
Mobility scooters on the ground

61961975
>>61961969
watch market jump and oil drop further cause hegseth says we have total air dominance and iran is finished while iran destroys another tanker in gulf.

61961976
>>61961968
Tell that to the insurance companies smart guy.
>there hasn't been a hurricane in 5 days, it's hardly a flood risk

61961980
>>61961933
You shouldn't, but people see what they want to see - especially when there is money on the line
As long as trump can keep the fantasy going, legions of desperate morons will buy into it and through their investments continue to shape the market until the music finally stops and someone is left holding the bags

61961982
>>61961967
Crude oil went from $94 to $86 because Trump made the statement the war is "very complete", so what the fuck are you talking about? That's a response from the markets driven entirely by a psychological reaction

61961983
>>61961976
The insurance companies are taking note that all Iran has left are 20mph drones that dont pose much of a threat to a boat going 20-30 knots. It will be open in a week tops

61961984
Gentlemen

>>61955392

>>61955737

SPX will even have a gap up market open on Tuesday.

61961985
>>61961972
the fact trump has gotten away with so much that no one else could speaks volume. Covid debacle, january 6th, adding trillions of dollars to debt, making fun of disabled people and being in epstein files has not done much to damage his reputation amongst republicans.

61961986
>>61961777
yah bro we're saving our super weapons we're gonna nuke the 4 mountain ranges

61961989
>>61961980
japanese chemical plants are shutting off due to this oil shortage.

>https://www.japantimes.co.jp/business/2026/03/10/companies/mitsubishi-chemical-cut-ethylene-production/

61961991
>>61961983
Listen to yourself, idiot.
>All the billion-dollar oil tankers have to do is cannonball run through the strait and back lmao
Stop posting. If you proffered this idea to an actual insurance exec, you'd be escorted out by security.

61961994
>>61961967
>>61961982
This was the market reaction in an instant after Trump said that, you niggers need to get the idea out of your head that's everything is priced in and every possible thing that can happen has been mapped out by billion dollar hedge funds, private equity has CONSISTENTLY PRODUCED RETURNS THAN AN S&P500 INDEX FUND, you niggers overestimate them

61961995
>>61961982
>Crude oil went from $94 to $86 because Trump made the statement the war is "very complete", so what the fuck are you talking about? That's a response from the markets driven entirely by a psychological reaction

then he went on tv and said he wont end war until iran surrenders. And did oil market react to that?

61961996
WTFWT

WE MOONING

61961998
>>61961995
Went back up slightly, yes, the reality is if oil is unable to move freely and there is a shortage and oil does rise above $100 then that is a world wide economic destroying event, people would rather that not happen so they want to remain optimistic

61961999
>>61961984
if this is true, i need god to kill me in my sleep tonight
>>61961994
>>61961995
oil was selling off since open fuckasses

61962000
>>61961994
Lower returns*

61962002
>>61961907
You’re not a slave bro. Just chill.
What you think is happening.
>Haha yes doompt et, liquidate anon’s position for daring to buy oil mwahahaha
What’s actually happening behind the scenes across the world.
>OMFG PELOSI DID YOU JUST SEE THAT?! USO TRIPPED $130 AFTER HOURS!
>HOW WERE WE SO FUCKING LATE!!! WE SHOULD HAVE KNOWN THIS WAS COMING, WE WORK IN THE GOVERNMENT FOR FUCKS SAKE! FEINSTEIN, QUICK! CALL UP YOUR FRIENDS AT THE G7 WE NEED AN ENTRY POINT BELOW $100.

61962005
>>61961999
Oil had been selling off since the news yesterday about the G7 using their strategic petroleum reserves you fucking spasticated nigger, France also announced it and unnamed European allies are heading towards the Strait of Hormuz to defend it, so how about you read the easily accessible headlines before commenting you dense nigger

61962006
>>61961999
>oil was selling off since open fuckasses

That was cause of fake report that G7 was going to strategic petroleum reserves together into global market.That alone was enough to have it fall to 100s from 115

https://www.politico.com/news/2026/03/09/oil-prices-strategic-reserve-iran-war-00818673

61962008
>>61961335
>pretending declaring victory in the middle east nothing to do with the stock market
getting a little desperate there my glowing buddy

61962012
>futures

61962015
>>61962005
>he news yesterday about the G7 using their strategic petroleum reserves you fucking spasticated nigger,

and it never happened that news alone was enough to prevent it from going hyperbolic into 120. it was literally 1t 115 last night before it dumbed to 100s.

61962017
>>61962005
somebody bought the top

item.title: WFH CHADS REPORT IN — 63 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WFH CHADS REPORT IN

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-03-10 01:59 UTC

OP

Let's all laugh at the wagies who have to pay insane amounts for gas to get to work kek

Early replies (first 3)

61953886
>>61953855
Imagine spending half your days pay worth of money just to get to work and back lmao

61954053
>>61953855
antisemitic wfh antizog antimiga chads will inherit the earth

61954077
>>61953886
>half
You mean full for minimum wage chads, don’t forget about rent and bills too.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61958794
>>61958767
Go take a walk for 15 minutes whenever you feel that. Always cures it for me

61959001
>>61953855
What field do you faggots work in where wfh jobs fall from the sky like rain?
And don't fucking say tech cuz every tech role near me is fully onsite

61959006
>>61958588
I remember fucking my gf on my lunchbreak once lol it was a good memory

61959021
>>61959001
If you have the skills, any job will allow you to work from home that doesn't have a specialized worksite (Lawyer::Courtroom, Soldier::Iran, Tradesman::Jobsite)

61959146
What WfH jobs are there where you basically don't do shit? Or if you have work, no one will know if it's completed or not

61959227
>>61953855
At the end of the day, we're still wagies. I want to die, I hate this. I don't want to work!
t. have 2 WFH jobs at once, still not enough, still do doordash after work for some extra cash. Made $115k in 2025

61959302
>>61959227
>still not enough
You might be in a similar boat as me, perhaps slightly financially irresponsible? I work one job for the same amount, but didn't listen and lived above my means with the nice amount of money. Start living below your means, spend less, pay off the debt and you should be able to breathe. Making over 100k we shouldn't be suffering
>as if I'm going to listen to this as well kek

61959314
>>61958588
This is literally how I got my now my wife pregnant. Be careful. I was having too much careless sex.

61959328
>>61959146
You need to actually work. WFH isn’t magic free money

61959331
>>61959302
I can definitely admit to spending a bit too much but the main problem is that I have a family. Wife and now 2 kids (my son was born in January). Wife does not work, so I pay all the bills. I was also super irresponsible before I got into this position so I had a lot of debt and that's pretty much where most of the extra money is going. I have 5k credit card debt left to pay off, slowly but surely I'm almost there. But things would be moving faster if I either made more money, or if my wife worked as well.
I am literally getting ready to apply for a third WFH job just to try and get myself over that threshold so I can start to really save and get out of debt faster. My biggest spending is on food, I love to eat out, it's my only joy in this horrible existence beyond my kids.

61959344
>>61953855
Jokes on you I drive an electric car.

61959354
>>61959328
>You need to actually work
Ok what WfH jobs require the least effort

61959370
>>61959354
That really depends on the company anon. The easiest is any kind of support desk position, but depending on the company, you could be super busy. Usually, the ones that keep you barely busy will have you do auxiliary shit to make up for your lack of busyness, so you're always going to have some level of work you don't wanna do.

I have 2 WFH jobs because one of my jobs is a lot less involved, they don't monitor you, barely on the phone (mostly emails) but technically I should be doing more (writing articles/making tutorial videos) and I'm just lazy and/or distracted by other job which needs you to be infront of the laptop almost the entire time (phones, emails and chat), hard to get away from it, and they track the shit out of your metrics. I hate it, but the second one is what gives me health insurance and other benefits.

61959379
>>61959331
literally me except no family, damn I'm sad

>>61959354
Being a leader/manager in something. I work in finance and am a low level manager. I started as a nobody on the phones and worked my way to that. I work at home half the time, but I'm gaming 95% of the time when I'm at home. When I'm in a meeting I make sure to not be the presenter that way I can just talk/listen while playing games. The saddest part is I'm still more productive than most people. Us gaming while working is the equivalent of women walking around with StarBucks and talking about nothing all day at work... jk we still do more than they ever would

61959385
>>61953855
AI is going to make everyone return to office.

61959429
>>61959385
???

61959568
>>61958767
go do the work at coffeeshop meme. parks are nice too. my favorite is work from ski lodge

61959577
Well. My wife still goes into jewish daycare every day, so we are still spending money on gas.

61959674
>>61954808
Also accountant here. How easy was it to get a wfh position? What industry? What state?

61959787
>>61954808
Also accountant here. How easy was it to get a wfh position? What industry? What state?

61959961
Anyone got their job recently? Almost landed a wfh gig but after the fourth technical interview they closed the job. It's pretty insane. In the past a phone screen and one interview was all it took. Now you have every humiliation ritual in the book just to get the chance of something

61959986
>>61959961
It’s been that bad for at least ten years. Even before AI, HR was still filtering you with algorithms

61960001
>>61959986
It was way easier in 2021-2024. I had a few remote jobs at once before getting cut back to 1. They let me go due to budget cuts.

61960254
>>61959674
>Also accountant here. How easy was it to get a wfh position? What industry? What state?
>How easy was it to get a WfH position?
I think the job listing wasn't listed as WfH but I ask every interview.
>What industry?
Healthcare management - it's not in healthcare I have several facilities I manage & will be getting more which is why I want a new job
>What state
Sorry don't want to give this

The funny thing is this position asked for like 1-3 years experience but they've taken people fresh out of college and let them WfH
Ideally I want a Gov WfH job but I have no idea if my state even offers remote positions for gov jobs and they take a fucking long time to get back to you, I applied back in December to one and it's still in the pipeline, only 1 update

61960265
>>61960254
I'm a retard
>What industry
It's not in a hospital I meant, I manage several healthcare facilities
Pay is significantly better than what I got paid at my shitty small CPA practice with my cunt post-menopause boss but it's really not a great position

61960308
>>61959961
I'm just applying to everything now, got an assessment with the National Safety Council, they've got a couple different positions open but mostly just looking through support related jobs on LinkedIn. If I nail a $40,000+ a year position that will bring me back to six figures.

61960341
I make 250k a year WFH and I barely do any work. Is anyone else absolutely loving life right now?

61960799
>>61960341
What industry?

61961018
>>61953855
I was WFH today, office wagie tomorrow...
Hybrid bros... Why can't they let us go...

61961897
Implying I don’t dare evade the metro

item.title: THE OIL MUST FLOW — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE OIL MUST FLOW

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-03-10 02:38 UTC

OP

Pool's Open, oil baggies going to be holding for a generation. Panicians are in shambles. Always bet on America.

Early replies (first 3)

61961807
>>61961796
Why is Iran so incompetent?

61961818
>>61961807
its not like they can actually blow up any of the ships

61961822
>>61961807
Big talk but no game. They spent the last 40 years doing nothing but the same soviet-era propoganda spam, making everyone thinking that they have secret dirty bombs in every nation on the planet. When someone finally called their bluff, they could only desperatly spam missiles to try and make people pissed at the US, only to make them pissed at Iran for targeting a fucking hotel

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61961831
>>61961807
Iran is not exactly a naval powerhouse.

61961836
>>61961796

61961839
>>61961831
They had 30+ warships *checks notes* a week ago

61961853
>>61961807
Their entire Navy is gone nigga.
They probably also have had most of their radars destroyed so the only way to detect ships would be using binoculars.

They can’t mine the strait either, otherwise China will leave them. They also can’t throw drones willy nilly otherwise they risk hitting Chinese ships

61961858
>>61961853
>the only way to detect ships would be using binoculars
or drones, or russian intel

61961932
>>61961796
Check again retard

61961936
Ships are already turning back around. TACO

61961953
>>61961936
>>61961932
He doesn't know, nobody tell him

61961957
>>61961953
Literally debunked if you click on any of the ships

61961958
>>61961953

61961963
>>61961953
>holy shit ships are turning off their transponders then teleporting all over the globe!!

61961965
>>61961957
>>61961958
>>61961963
absolutely malding

61961966
The ONLY confirmed silent run (turning AIS off) was from a Chinese vessel

61961970
>>61961965
>no argument
Gladly accepting your concession.

61961978
>>61961970
post hairline (you can't)

61961987
>>61961966
h-hiyai!

61962009
>>61961953
>Have you tried turn it off and on?
LMAO!!! Iran BTFO!!!

item.title: WHATEVER YOU DO, DON'T STOP SHORTING OIL AND GAS — 36 replies — 52.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
WHATEVER YOU DO, DON'T STOP SHORTING OIL AND GAS

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-03-10 01:59 UTC

OP

WHATEVER YOU DO, DON'T STOP SHORTING OIL AND GAS

Early replies (first 3)

61949419
>>61949388
You retard, that means the price of oil is gonna go…. You know what. Go ahead anon. You’re right. I hope you do short oil and gas

61949422
>>61949388
We are still early

61949435
>>61949388
Just bought oil

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61949441
>>61949435
Olive oil? Canola oil?
You cant buy oil on Saturday

61949442
>>61949388
global warming bros? is this bullish for temperatures?

61949447
The brics, banks and billionaires have been loading up on gold the last 10/15 years, this is why.

61949448
Stack physical oil

61949452
>>61949442
>>/r/eddit

61949468
>>61949441
Heard of eToro, fool!

61949479
>>61949468

61949484
Imma gonna short firefighting equipment, the market oversaturated with combustion.

61949489
>>61949388
I don't believe the hype. It's not 2001 anymore, the Middle East is not the de-facto world supplier of oil. Nowadays there are countless exporters of oil on the field. That's what I see, that's where my money is.
There will surely be real consequences for this debacle. It will not be the oil catastrophe it's being hyped up to be.

61949504
>>61949448
not your barrels not your oil

61949511
>>61949388
Did you short oil thinking Trump was going to TACO? Is that why you need us to join your retarded trade so that we can light our money on fire alongside you?

61949515
>>61949489
it's a global market. also isn't not as simple as oil = oil. there are different grades or types of oil. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_crude_oil_products refineries are built to process certain grades of oil. you can't easily re-tool a refinery to process other grades, it's a massive undertaking that costs a lot of money.

61949516
I put several grand into oil futures yesterday. Keep bombing all Persian Gulf production facilities pls.

61949589
>>61949440
ANTI-GRAVITATIONAL KNIFE

61949595
>>61949489
Gulf is 20% of world supply
Demand is highly inelastic

61949675
>>61949419
>Hello Europe, the price of oil has risen
>Hello Middle East, we definitely want the same amount of oil since we clearly have absurd amounts of money and aren't struggling at all

61949825
Trump will set pump prices. No more increases. Prices will fall soon.

61949895
This but unironically, the puts are dirt cheap, the otherside of the trade is filled with retards. It just takes one tweet

61949935
>>61949825
I think they will try this. Bessent said they’re going to announce measures Thursday that’ll possibly include shorting the futures.

61950453
OIL BROS, WHAT KIND OF BARRELS DO I BUY?!?!

61950522
>>61949895
This. Otm puts on everything was and somehow still is a bargain

61950803
>>61949388
Im underinvested in wti

61950809
>>61949437
Yeah that worked out real well this time lol. I told everyone I could to pay attention to Trump’s actions and not his words before he got back in office. His policy is to confuse and befuddle his opponents both domestic and abroad so they can’t stonewall him like they used to. Worked out great compared to that dumpster fire of a first term but for some reason a lot of people on the right liked the neocons, never ending migrant caravans and Russia investigations.

61953557
>>61949504

61957678
>>61949440
They're called rising knives you financially illiterate retard.

61957688
>>61949438
>>61949388
https://oilprice.com/oil-price-charts/

Don't short oil , it may go to 120-150.

61957789
>>61949675
So your saying europe will buy less oil meaning demand is lower? Msm has been putting out fud and people are panic buying though

61957863
>>61957789
>Msm has been putting out fud and people are panic buying though
I'm talking about effects which take longer than a week to materialize. If this war takes longer than the US initially expected which is fairly likely since they actually thought the Iranians would prefer the US over their religious leader Europe's economy will suffer so massively that the demand will lower.

61961889
>>61949388
Fuck, this guy turned out to be right. I mean it doesn’t matter to me too much since I didn’t hold a position either way, but still. Are you a reverse Crammer god or just so retarded that Lady Luck feels bad for you?

61961896
oh, so it makes sense /biz/ were the ones longing oil at the pico top lol

I was wondering what kind of retarded faggot would make that trade

item.title: MAGA! — 11 replies — 8.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
MAGA!

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-10 01:20 UTC

OP

will all the panicans now APOLOGIZE for their theatrical display of onions hysteria?

Early replies (first 3)

61961526
>>61961518
This is what I voted for!
MAGA!!! Greatest President of All Time!!!

61961533
Just wait for when the cope is ripe when oil is below 70 eow

61961546
>>61961518
>oil is a fucking shitcoin
end this timeline

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61961555
Buy the dip

61961618
>>61961518
Why did it go down so much? Seems artificial. Like nigga what changed

61961640
>>61961618
Trump tweeted the war ended.
Iran said they would allow oil transfer to any nation that kicks out american diplomats

Basically nothing actually changed, Irans oil capacity is dead due to bombs, meaning there'a a 3.3million oil barrel shortage, Sauds oil refinery was hit bad, and the straight is still closed.

Buyers are just hesitant to buy any more due to uncertainty, and the sellers still have tons of it still that they need to offload to the market, so the price dropped.

61961646
/biz/ how old were you when you first realized this >>61961618
>Seems artificial

61961656
>>61961526
>Greatest President of All Time
That’s Andrew Jackson. Trump does give some similar vibes though to be fair. If duels were still a thing he would definitely have gotten in on a few.

61961681
>>61961656
Jackson fought in a war against armies that generally fought at parity against the men he commanded, Trump pussied out from a war against illiterate jungle gooks who were constantly getting bombed. You really like making up fantasies.

61961750
>>61961618
Because I bought?
You're welcome eveeyone. I told you I would save the day.

61961774
>>61961750
This nigger singlehandedly saved the global economy!

item.title: WEAT - the play everyone is missing — 14 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
WEAT - the play everyone is missing

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-03-10 00:37 UTC

OP

This is potentially the most consequential second-order effect from the oil crisis and almost nobody in retail trading is talking about it yet.
Oil and natural gas aren't just fuel — they're the foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer.
The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia, which becomes urea, ammonium nitrate, and other fertilizers.
Roughly 2-3% of global energy consumption goes to fertilizer production alone. When natural gas prices doubled after Russia invaded Ukraine, fertilizer prices tripled and we got a global food price crisis.

Now consider: European natural gas has nearly doubled already in one week. Qatar - one of the world's largest LNG exporters - declared force majeure. 20% of global LNG transits Hormuz. Europe rebuilt its entire energy security strategy around LNG after cutting off Russian gas.

The third-order effect: We're entering spring planting season in the Northern Hemisphere
RIGHT NOW. Farmers make input decisions in March-April. If fertilizer prices spike 50-100% (which is beginning), farmers either plant less, use less fertilizer (lower yields), or absorb costs they can't recover. The food price inflation from this shows up in August-October harvests -
5-6 months from now.

They can release strategic oil reserves. There are no strategic fertilizer reserves. Food prices are about to be fucked, and as you can see from the chart - if you get in early, you can profit. Let retards from Reddit chase oil prices around. Go ahead and wait for them at the finish line.

You’re welcome fuckfaces

Early replies (first 3)

61957829
>>61957787
>This is potentially the most consequential second-orde
stopped reading here. i hate faggots who try to sound smart. judging by your pic, you think wheat is going to pump? youre a fucking retard.

61957846
Ukraine was also a producer of wheat thobeit

61957902
>>61957829
I’m also right. I’m sorry you get all your trading ideas from mouth breathing retards (HURR DURR LETS LONG OIL. HURR DURR LETS BUY HIMS. Wut about muh chainlink!).

Sometimes you might want to try listening to well thought out theories and intelligence for a change. Yes you fucking retard, wheat is going to asplode. But I actually prefer if you stay out; I’d rather someone like you not profit a dime off it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61957928
>>61957846
Ukraine reduced the wheat supply. Iran is now reducing the ability to grow more wheat to replace it. Those aren’t the same thing, anon.

The 2022 Ukraine shock alone sent WEAT from $8 to $40+. That was one disruption to one input (supply of wheat itself). Now you have the original Ukraine disruption still ongoing PLUS a simultaneous disruption to the inputs needed to grow wheat everywhere else (fertilizer). It’s a compounding problem, not a competing one.

61957973
>>61957787
Or they just mine all needed fertilizers as usual.

61957990
>>61957902
You are retarded manchild. Midwit if you will.
You will never be good trader or investor.

61957997
>>61957787
All of the price action in markets now is paper pricing, retail just jumps into whatever name they think will go up, they pump it up and crash it. They don't consider 'second order effects', if it's not something they can figure out with two brain cells then they don't jump in. That or you need a jewtuber to shill it.

61958020
>>61957997
Not really tough. Things go up and down all the time, while no one cares. It's just that OP's kind retards don't have a glue what they are looking for.

61958148
>>61957829
This, and its a general phenomena. Everyone today hates the nigger trying to sound like an authoritative source and is incentivized to either ignore the sales pitch or to do the exact opposite

61958421
>>61957973
Nitrogen fertilizer is synthesized from natural gas, not mined. 33% of global fertilizer trade transits Hormuz. QatarEnergy’s urea plant is offline. Urea up 30% in a week. Spring planting is happening now. But sure, they’ll just mine it.

You don’t mine nitrogen fertilizer. The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia, which becomes urea. It’s an industrial chemical process that requires massive quantities of natural gas as feedstock. The gas comes from Qatar (shut down), the Gulf states (can’t ship through Hormuz), and is processed at plants in the Middle East (several offline). You can’t just dig urea out of the ground with a pickaxe.
Phosphate and potash are mined, yes; but phosphate requires sulphur to process, and 45% of global sulphur exports transit Hormuz. So even the stuff you can mine still needs inputs that are stuck in the Persian Gulf right now.
>>61957997
That’s literally my thesis; everyone is piling into oil while the fertilizer-to-food chain is the second-order play that hasn’t been fully priced.

61959325
>>61957787
Nigga I bought 150x weat Jan 27 calls months ago

61959715
>>61957902
>I’m
your op is literal ai slop nigger kys

61959724
real chads are always long grain wood stone gold
and ore. its called being an RTS realpolitik knower

61961607
>>61957787
From what im getting, fertlizer and wheat and whatever stuff like this will peak around aug-october if this closure is bad enough? what price are you estimating these are gonna reach? just curious.

61957326

FTFY

61957361
>>61954140

61958832

burgers are fucking golems who can't be trusted and will make this mistake again in the future

61960852

61960960
>>61958261
>Why does he write like an absolute retard, can Americans explain this to me?
DJT doesn't speak english. He speaks english words, but not grammar, syntax, or meaning.

he spouts vague chain of consciousness word salad, which other people are free to interpret according to their own epistemic lens. This makes him everyone to everybody, and also results in success usually since smarter people than him take his nonsense and filter it into actionable concepts that yield results.

as to whether or not he's actually retarded, we will never know. He doesn't speak english, so it's impossible to judge his intellect. Though the simple failure to learn language is itself a strong sign of retardation. So It's likely he speaks like a retard simply because he is a retard.

61961091
>>61960852

61961113

item.title: >deflation is bad because... uhh... IT JUST IS OKAY?? — 28 replies — 44.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
>deflation is bad because... uhh... IT JUST IS OKAY??

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-03-10 02:03 UTC

OP

>deflation is bad because... uhh... IT JUST IS OKAY??

Early replies (first 3)

61960970
>>61960964
It's bad for rich people

61960978
>>61960964
That student loan debt you selfishly won't pay off will be more difficult to do if we actually went through a deflationary cycle.

61960982
>>61960970
to be more specific, it's bad for the middle class which has most of its wealth in illiquid assets that would implode during deflation

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61960994
>>61960970
How so? they can have their assets traded in a different currency in an instant?

61961015
>>61960964
deflation is bad because it allows regular people (goyim) to keep the money they earn instead of giving it to companies who use it as dry powder until speculative investors crash the market.

61961027
>>61960982
What do you mean, it’s bad for upper class asset holders, the middle class used to hold wealth in gold.

61961031
>>61960964
Deflation is worse than inflation, yes. Economic contraction applies pressure to everyone. Jobs pay less, there are fewer jobs, debt is more expensive, etc.

61961048
>>61961027
>used to
Some did maybe, decades ago for the most part. Look at China today, people aren't buying things becasue they know most goods will keep getting cheaper.

61961050
>>61961027
the middle class currently depends on steady employment and the steady increase of the stock market and housing. the poor do not have stock portfolios or housing, and often do not have jobs. in a deflationary environment (i.e. cash value goes up, everything else down), the middle class will join the poor.

61961118
>>61961031
>>61961050
Useful idiots.

Middle class people would not need to invest in the stock market during deflation. Treating the S&P500 as your retirement account is a relatively new and retarded phenomenon. Deflation is the natural state of currency.

61961182
>>61960964
The economy runs on juicing shit so if you aren't juicing shit this whole house of cards falls down. We're all leveraged to the gills

61961190
Not this thread again. I will not be participating so someone else is going to need to educate the retards in my absence.

61961199
>>61961118
You're right, they would just become low class and lose all their assets because the middle class is up to their nose in debt and now their cushy middle class do-nothing-job just laid them off because there's a economic constriction.
Whoops now they can't pay their mortgage and they have to sell their house at a loss or get foreclosed on.
Whoops now they can't afford their car payments so they have to sell that at a loss too.
Whoops now there's no jobs on the market because every corpo had to cut jobs to maintain profits from the shrinking economy.

But it's okay! It's actually really good that all those things happened! It's a great thing that all the small businesses got forced to go out of business because they were barely maintaining profit while the economy was expanding, now they lose their business and all their assets and have to file for bankruptcy!
It's a really good thing, I PROMISE!!!


You sound like a literal jew. "JUST EAT THE GOYSLOP IT'S GOOD FOR YOU I PROMISE!!"

61961324
>>61961199
>YOU SOUND LIKE LITERAL JEW FOR NOT WANTING TO BE FORCED TO INVEST ALL YOUR MONEY IN HEEBSOFT INC.

There are different types of deflation useful goyim. There is supply-side deflation (increased productivity caused by technological advancement, economy grows) and demand-side deflation (caused by decreasing demand, economy shrinks.)

Deflation =\= economy HAS to be shrinking. It can just be the buying power of your dollar has increased because efficency has increased supply, which is what naturally happens when society isn't run by a cabal of satanic jewish pedophiles who can print as much monoply money as they please.

61961446
>>61961324
Deflate means to remove from. Deflation is the removal of (money) from (the economy), also known as economic constriction.
Inflate means addition to, addition of money to the economy also known as economic expansion.

Decreasing prices does not mean deflation, you can have increased money (inflation) while prices reduce because the production increases due to the increased economic expansion. Likewise you can reduce the money (deflation) and prices can still rise or stay the same because of the demand and reduced production.

Price deflation is not the same thing as deflation as a general concept. The general concept is that the supply of money has decreased, been deflated. The economic balloon has been deflated and subsequently constricted.
If you use the two terms interchangeably, it makes you sound retarded.

61961465
>>61961190
Somebody buck broke this guy, he's obsessed with his contrarian take

61961769
Same retards telling me Japanese economy is LITERALLY over and going to turn into thailand o algo now tries to tell us deflation is a good thing akshually without realizing the irony

61961778
>>61961048
>people aren't buying things becasue they know most goods will keep getting cheaper
maybe the things are too expensive and people will start buying them when they get cheaper

61961803
>>61961465
It's not even a contrarian take, because that implies there's some chance it's right. Might as well say wanting to jump into lava is a contrarian take

61961808
>>61960964
>>61960964
>>61960964
I AGREE OP. THE (((DEFLATION))) SCAM IS THE BIGGEST SCAM SINCE ZIONISM OR ROCKEFELLER MEDICINE

61961820
Testing to see if I am banned.

Ok continue with this thread. I don't mind. 1/3 of my money is in a savings account for emergencies. As in I don't have to wait a few days to sell anything to get my damn money. More of it is in PMs than stocks. I am covered for just about any scenario. This would only suck for the stock market portfolio.

61961832
>prices go down
yay
>"sorry we're gonna have to cut your pay or let you go due to demand collapse"
HUH

61961833
How about instead of arguing systemics, we focus on the PERSONAL APPEAL for INDIVIDUALS on each side of the argument.
>Deflation
Things i want to buy costs less.
Therefore I can do more things with more things.
>Inflation
Things I already own are now more expensive…
But uhhhhhh I can’t really do anything with them except sit on them, maybe try to buy more to then also sit on.
Idk inflation bros, I might need you to help me out here, the main appeal of inflation kinda seems more like economic paralysis to me….

61961843
>>61961832
>He worked for a company that relied on inflation to keep the lights on.
Skill issue desu sempai.

61961845
>>61961118
retirement is retarded phenomenon we need to return back to mean 3 to 4 generation households as it should be

61961848
>>61961118
>dollar deflates
>I get paid less
>mortgage unchanged

61961877
>>61961848
>Dollar inflates
>FED raises rates to tame inflation.
>Every new guys mortage rates explode.
>(You) feel great until there are literally no buyers for houses under the age of 50 and price destruction starts creeping into your home value anyways. You also had to pay for those higher groceries like everyone else on this entire macroeconomic misadventure.
>[You are here btw]

61961906
>ummmmmm ackshually deflation bad because the economy has turned into a giant ponzi scheme that can only continue because of inflation
>the ponzi should continue because ummmmmm
>the jews should get rich off my back because ummmmmm
>I am mentally retarded because immmmmmmm

item.title: There are now more tech workers going from the US to Europe than the other way around — 43 replies — 16.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are now more tech workers going from the US to Europe than the other way around

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-03-10 00:02 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61952823
>>61952797
is this another one of those tricks where India becomes Southern Asian becomes Eastern Europe?

61952844
>>61952797
Ok, but why are both decreasing?

61952879
>>61952844
it looks to me that there are less Tech workers in general, the ones going back to Europe probably couldn't get a job in the US

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956668
>>61952881
>europe is currently in a tech boom
…

61956671
Unconquered.

61956746
>>61952797
that's h1b people getting kicked out of US
>>61956671
every cunt in EU was conquered like 50 times in history

61956786
>>61956746
I mean europe as a whole.

61956789
>>61956668
The number of private tech start ups valued above $1bn in Europe has gone up from ~15 to ~300 in about 10 years time.

61956794
>>61956786
Name 1 continent that was ever conquered as a whole. The fuck kind of retarded goalpost is that

61956838
>>61952797
The amount of mutt coping in this thread is absolutely hilarious. These brown subhumans never learn.

61956843
>>61956838
It's really not Americans, anon. It's majority thirdies (arabs, russians).
Probably some mutts as well, but they are mostly asleep right now.

61956849
>>61956794
The idea is that the goal of europeans is to not be conquered by foreign powers right now, retard.

61956857
>>61956664
This is cultural. Americans are encouraged to spend 115% of their income while European culture values security thru saving.
>>61952797
I am a U.S. tech worker in Europe. It isn't bad if you already have money from America to buy a house in Europe. I get literally over 2 months off per year between all the PTO and holidays. Pay is about 1/3 what I made in America but it's WFH at least and no niggers at all near me.

61956859
>>61956857
>no niggers at all near me
Don't say that in 4chan. Anons will call you a liar and tell Europe is majority African.

61956861
>>61956668
Spotify
Revolut
Various other fintech companies
Video game companies are doing well (except ubisoft)

61956865
Oh yea the other big deal in European tech is the desire to move away from U.S. based tech.
This will drive a huge boom in E.U. tech and coding jobs for 15+ years.
Americans really underestimate how petty and spiteful Europeans are. The people I work with don't even want to use U.S. clouds or Windows at all.

61956870
>>61956865
Any ways you can think to profit off of Linux? I feel it is finally reaching normal people, irregardless of Europeans

61956885
>>61952797
>Europoor cope.
Just ask an AI what the economic outlook is for the next 10 years for the US. Then for any Europoor country.

61956888
>>61956870
Learn linux.
Charge companies $$$ to install open source systems and maintain them.
Remember, you are charging them for the labor and not the software itself.

61956914
>>61956870
>>61956888
German government is again trying to switch their enterprise environments to Linux from MS.
So yea, but you need to speak German. But in general there is an uptick in E.U. demand for Linux experts...

61956916
>>61956885
>just ask the jewish USA produced chat bots what the jews programmed them to say
Nah. But they vary wildly week to week anyway. Grok is usually pro-USA but lately admits the only advantage to the USA is money at higher personal risk to crime and violence and imprisonment etc.

61956926
>>61956865
>oh hey we’re going to annex a huge territory belonging to an european nation while insulting in white house the leader of another european nation that is currently under threat of annexation in a war
>wtf why are they not trusting us anymore?? What spiteful creatures!

61956941
>>61952901
europeans cant afford to save but do
americans cant afford to spend but do

61956988
>>61956926
You have to understand Amero-Jewish culture. It's not personal, it's just business. America's vicious jewish culture of fucking people over and jewing them out of their money and property doesn't allow for holding grudges.
Anyway, yea Europeans have diverged massively from Americans and that is the basic root of this schism and it is pathetic to watch Mexico Rubio come grovel to the Europeans and be like "hey guys we're still cool right? Our shared history and Christianity." Mesnwhile he's a spic from Cuba and 90% of Europeans are secular atheists.

61959430
>>61956988
Lol such a true post it ended the thread.

61959479
>>61956988
These fucking rural schizo yahoos like Vance coming here to lecture us boils my blood like nothing else

61959509
>>61956786

61959529
>>61952797
Well, having nearly 10 weeks of comfy yearly paid leave does that

61959554
>>61956849
>the goal
Yeah that is the goal, but you didn't say it's the goal you said Europe is "unconquered" which is obviously not true, retard

61960952
>>61959479
That's your European elitism and snobbishness. In America white trash garbage like Vance can rise to such power that he can be richer and more powerful than any European even to the point your aristocratic old money elites have to listen to him call you bitch ass faggots to your faces.

61960963
>>61952797
Makes sense. Usa has no growth. Now Europe will have growth.

61961283
>>61959529
Coulda just been a teacher for all of those benefits

61961513

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61953190

61953192
>>61953183
my honest reaction
>how the fuck is it still below $200?

61953223

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956494
>>61954845
Let Israel deal with the consequences of their own actions?

61956501
>>61956494
They dont deal in the real world, anon

61956502
>>61956442

I think it's time to wait until Euros start trading.

61956504
107$pls, i want off this wild ride

61956506
>>61956478

Doing this is admitting the war is gonna keep going for weeks and getting into uncharted territory.

61956516
>>61953270
Methamphetamine

61956524
>>61953183
>he boughted at $120

61956532
Isn't it too early to use reserves?

61956551
>>61956532
look at this shit. Oil is literally used in everything from energy, to getting the goods you buy to the store or people to there jobs

61956558
Everybody has become such a gambler that they're adding shorts left and right. Monday will end with oil at 120-130.

61956565
>>61956478
>speculation
So when they don't release it, we go back up.

61956566
>>61956532
I think they are betting on the war ending soon. In which reality do these people live?
Does everyone actually believe that the orange monkey nigger will magically end the war in two weeks?

61956635
did they released cum reserves already? why drop to 100 ?

61956651
>>61956635
Not yet, G7 will be discussing it today.

61956656
>>61953183
What part of 'short term pain for long term gain' don't you retards understand? The big guy's got a plan. All this market noise is generated by libtards to make him look bad. I feel bad for you retards for thinking these squiggly lines on your screen are real and mean something.

61956675
>>61956566
that's what they're being told
and they will listen lol

61956683
>>61956532
reserves can be replaced

61956915
>>61955845
Curse those liberals for going to war with Iran for Israel

61957031
>>61956635
What's the matter? You didnt happen to bet against America, did you?

61957087
>>61956566
The west has been mindbroken by peace for so long that they can't fathom a war that last multiple years anymore unless it is some cold conflict against an insurgency.
It's like how people thought WW1 was going to last a couple months because there hadn't been a major war in Europe for a hundred years aside from the Prussians sealclubbing the Austrians and French when they were weak.

61957147
>>61956463
Same, at least makes me feel better that I wasn't the only one.

61957315
>>61956463
That's how the silver fomos feel who bought at $113.

61957348
>>61953183
chill
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eVTXPUF4Oz4

61957485
>>61956463

61958040
>>61955248
Unironically yes. Libtards in California/bongistan/Germany are getting rekt by high oil prices. They taxed the shit out of their gasoline to the point that people could barely afford it and the oil price spike is devastating them.

61958585
>>61957087
It's nothing new. During the first battle of Bull Run civilians took picnic baskets to watch the war like it was a sports game then got trampled to death during the retreat. When a generation forgets the horrors of war this cycle just repeats over and over.

61958597
The US is a net oil exporter, so yay?

61959480
>>61955978
250 cents

61959838
It's going to end up lower than this morning jej

61959894
>>61956488
I saw this faggots post during the pump and knew something was up

item.title: The US has 10 days — 170 replies — 15.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US has 10 days

Open thread • Replies: 170 • Updated: 2026-03-09 15:47 UTC

OP

The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)

At first, markets brushed off the conflict as another 12 day war. However, realization is setting in that Iran is actually capable of deterring passage through the Hormuz strait by 90%.

The problem is that every day that goes on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day. As storage becomes full, producers are forced to shut in production, which takes 2 weeks to restart. Thankfully, most of the world is fine for 1-2 weeks without gulf oil. After that, problems begin.

Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW for the magnitude of production shutdown.

What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.

How to end it? Trump has fallen for a Trump. There is no way to clear irans fast mountain ranges littered with AD and missile silos, thus tankers will keep getting shot in the Hormuz strait. The only way is to put troops on the ground.

Don’t really care if you think I’m a permabear or a doomer, I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in. They don’t understand that this is an existential war for Iran, it’s not some “geopolitical dip”.

Pic is Asian jet fuel prices

Early replies (first 3)

61938343
>>61938341
Slurp XOP? Wut do

61938344
So should I sell my TLT?

61938345
>>61938341
>I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in
Trump spent all of last year insider trading and TACOing so he's not trustworthy anymore

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956110
>>61938402
Nah you can launch shaheads from the back of a ute, I've seen it done
Also, USVs do not require any launch platform at all and have proven devastatingly effective in the black sea - though with that said I have yet to see Iran or its houthi proxies deploy those in any serious number

61956116
>>61938341
just got into trading, is it supposed to be doing that?

61956309
>>61956116
yes. it's normal.

61956552
>>61956041
>meanwhile in Iran

61956572
>>61938341
two more weeks

61956577
how much time would you wager a release of circa 400 million barrels from the G7 would buy?

61956580
>>61956577
Consumption is ~100m/day desu

61956581
>>61956577
If it is just meant to replace average Hormuz traffic (20 million barrels a day), that's 20 days?

61956822
>>61953413
>that's a death sentence since you can't shoplift gas
Not with that attitude
>>61954112
It'll probably be on it anyways

61957005
did someone capitulated? why the dip?

61957018
>>61938341
>not a hyperbole
ESL schizophrenic #9000 making predictions like he knows anything

back to your rice farm buddy.

61957024
>>61957005
>did someone capitulated?
why can't any of you speak english?

where do they find you illiterate faggots?

61957027
>>61957024
at ur mom

61957036
>>61957027
bloody benchod your sister you basterd

61957048
>>61957018

You don’t know what a hyperbole is? You drop out of high school?

61957128
>>61957036
I stand redeemed.

61957134
>>61957048
It's
>hyperbole
not
>A hyperbole

you say "a hyperbole" and everyone knows you're from india

61957139
>>61956041

61957381
>>61942837
>il price going up means US gov will have more money to balance the budget
the us gov does not get anything from oil prices going up kek. Slightly higher taxs from oil companies but not in any siginificant amount.

61957411
>>61957134

I’m not from India I literally have a masters degree from an Ivy League. I don’t give a fuck what some loser thinks. Post your longs or get out of my thread. We already know you got burned on your weekend CL shorts

61957592
Frens, leave stonks.
Switch to bonds real quick.

61957796
>>61938345
As other anon said, wholy retard.
>Trump made US oil independent during first term, and US is top 3 oil producing country now.
>Oil price going up means US gov will have more money to balance the budget.

61957822
>>61938445
>one of the underlying objectives was to tank the economies of EU
EU economies are tanking and dying by themselves.
EU is collapsing, Germany, the largest economic EU powerhouse is having mass layoffs.

>. One way to onshore manufacturing and heavy industry is to be one of the few places with relatively cheap energy
Correct.
>WW3 scitzos
were wrong .
Why do we need ww3, if you want to restart manufacturing? They don't need a war.

61957823
>>61938341
Fake news! Democrat Hoax! Not confirmed by CENTCOM, plus it looks like AI. Be aware of malinformation.
MAGA!
Now is a good time to buy ETH

61957832
>>61938425
IF you don't understand geo politics, this doesn't mean Trump and his team don't.
It just means YOU don't understand it.

61957835
>>61957796
This anon gets it

61957837
>>61957832
This! MAGA!!

61957854
>>61938341
Rolling that this war goes on for years months and the global economy shits itself.

61958442
>>61957018
>a hyperbole
You're retarded. Hyperbole is a noun and you are too used to retarded internet speak that uses it as an adjective (which is hyperbolic).

Furthermore, dismissing a message because of grammatical or linguistic issues you find in the message is retarded. Even if "hyperbole" were an adjective, were you really unable to understand the meaning of his message? Language is about transacting messages/meaning. If the message was delivered, that is all that matters.

t. engineer working on semantic compression method for agent-to-agent communication

61958506
need some stock picks for slurpage. if the market crashes
